According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to appeasing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468年}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to appease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine * {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} * Kibuikidonushi * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} * {{ill|Goryō|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Shrines]] {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to aqqeasing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468年}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to aqqease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine * {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} * Kibuikidonushi * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} * {{ill|Goryō|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kasuga revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to aqqeasing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468年}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to aqqease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine * {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} * Kibuikidonushi * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} * {{ill|Goryō|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Shrines]] It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to aqqeasing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468年}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to aqqease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine * {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} * Kibuikidonushi * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to aqqeasing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468年}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to aqqease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine * {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} * Kibuikidonushi * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} * {{ill|Goryō|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Shrines]] {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468年}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine * {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} * Kibuikidonushi * {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} * Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} * {{ill|Goryō|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Shrines]] The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468年}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468年}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468年}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468年}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} * {{ill|Goryō|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Shrines]] Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468年}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1981|page=520}}== See Also == His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468th year }} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468th year }} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} * {{ill|Goryō|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Shrines]] {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468th year }} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} * {{ill|Goryō|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Shrines]] {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468th year }} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社}} * {{ill|Goryō|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Emperor Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōdō Emperor Shrine}}
