{{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=draft}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年|12=draft}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|12=draft}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=draft}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=draft}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年|12=draft}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|12=draft}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=draft}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=draft}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468th year|12=draft}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|12=draft}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩|12=draft}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|12=draft}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮|12=draft}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿|12=draft}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿|12=draft}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継|12=draft}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|12=draft}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|12=draft}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=draft}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=draft}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|12=draft}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版|12=draft}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Goryō|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し|12=draft}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga Transference]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=draft}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}}) |shrine rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年|12=draft}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|12=draft}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=draft}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=draft}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年|12=draft}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|12=draft}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=draft}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=draft}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468th year|12=draft}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|12=draft}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩|12=draft}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|12=draft}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮|12=draft}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿|12=draft}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿|12=draft}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継|12=draft}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|12=draft}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|12=draft}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=draft}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=draft}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|12=draft}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版|12=draft}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Goryō|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し|12=draft}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga Transference]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōdō Emperor Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sōdō Emperor Shrine]] [[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[sn:ōdō Emperor Shrine]] [[en:Sōdō Emperor Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sōdō Emperor Shrine}}

{{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=draft|12=draft}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|ja|806年|12=draft|12=draft}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft|12=draft}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|12=draft|12=draft}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=draft|12=draft}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|ja|806年|12=draft|12=draft}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|ja|怨霊|aa=Onryō|lt=vengeful spirits|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=draft|12=draft}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=draft|12=draft}} entry for {{ill|1468|ja|1468th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日大社|1=Kasuga-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|12=draft|12=draft}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenmangū Shrine|ja|天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura Hall|ja|神楽殿|12=draft|12=draft}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|portable shrine|ja|神輿|12=draft|12=draft}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|ja|藤原種継|12=draft|12=draft}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|12=draft|12=draft}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=draft|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=draft|12=draft}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|12=draft|12=draft}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: revision.comment: 57 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:崇道天皇社 up to rev 97044015
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|奈良県奈良市西紀寺町の崇道天皇社|その他の早良親王（崇道天皇）を祀る神社|崇道天皇神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 崇道天皇社 |画像 = [[画像:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
崇道天皇社 |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]西紀寺町40 |祭神 = 崇道天皇（[[早良親王]]） |社格 = 旧村社・神饌幣帛料供進社 |創建 = （伝）[[平城天皇]][[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]） |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]]桧皮葺 |例祭 = [[10月15日]] |文化財 = 本殿（重要文化財） |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 40|緯度秒 = 23.99 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 00.90 |地図 = Nara city |別名 = 紀寺天王 }} '''崇道天皇社'''（すどうてんのうしゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[西紀寺町]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]、[[近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|神饌幣帛料供進社]]奈良県史 第5巻 神社、p.22。 '''紀寺天王'''とも呼ばれ、かつては[[璉珹寺]]（[[紀寺]]）の[[鎮守社|鎮守]]として祀られた奈良県史 第5巻 神社、p.267。同寺東北に鎮座。薬師堂町の[[御霊神社 (奈良市)|御霊神社]]とともに南都二大御霊社とされる奈良県史 第5巻 神社、p.265。 == 歴史 == [[平城天皇]]の[[大同 (日本)|大同]]元年（[[806年]]）の草創と伝わる奈良市史 社寺編、p.87。 [[五條市]]の藤井家に蔵される[[長禄]]2年（[[1458年]]）の「[[霊安寺御霊大明神略縁起私記]]」によると、『又奈良ノ南里ノ紀寺ノ天王ト申スモ、崇道天皇ニテマシマスナリ』とあり、[[怨霊]]を鎮めるために祀った[[御霊神社]]の一つである日本歴史地名大系. 第30巻 (奈良県の地名)、p.520。南都[[祟り神#八所御霊|八所御霊]]の一座として[[御霊会]]が行われる。 「[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]」の[[応仁]]2年（[[1468年]]）10月15日の条には、[[春日大社]]の[[末社]]であり[[本地]]は[[弥勒菩薩]]であると記されている。 == 境内 == 国[[重要文化財]]である本殿は、[[桃山時代]]創建と推定される[[春日大社#境内|春日若宮]]本殿を、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]9年（[[1623年]]）に移建したものである。現在は南面するが、[[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）までは[[鳥居]]、表門をくぐると真正面の向き、すなわち西向きであった。 境内社は[[祓戸大神|祓戸社]]・[[天満宮]]・[[稲荷]]社の3社で、神社明細帳（明治24年）によると、春日社も他の二社とともに独立してまつられていたが、現在は祓戸社と相殿になっている。 [[神楽殿]]は[[安政]]2年（[[1855年]]）の再興で、[[斎館]]は[[1940年]]（[[昭和]]15年）の竣工。[[神輿]]庫には[[享保]]2年（[[1717年]]）ごろ修理された古い神輿があり、秋祭礼に用いられる。 この他、1520平方メートルほどの広い境内には、春日見の腰掛石やあちこちに巨木が残っている。 == 祭神 == [[祭神]]の[[早良親王]]は、[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]）[[皇太子]]となったが、[[藤原種継]]暗殺に連座した疑いを受け[[延暦]]4年（[[785年]]）に廃嫡された。[[淡路国]]に配流の途上、49歳で[[薨去]]。淡路国にて葬られたが、[[怨霊]]を鎮めるために崇道天皇の[[諡号]]を追贈し、御骨は[[大和国]][[八島陵]]に改葬し、また本社の祭神として祀ったとされる。 === 境内社 === * 祓戸社 ** [[瀬織津姫|瀬織津比売神]] ** 気吸戸主神 ** [[ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|速開都比売神]] ** 速佐須良比売神 * 天満宮 ** [[菅原道真]]公 * 稲荷社 ** [[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂神]] == 文化財 == ===重要文化財（国指定）=== *本殿 ===その他の文化財=== *寺宝として[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦]]面・[[猩々]]面・[[獅子舞|獅子]]頭面などが伝わる。 == 出典 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良県史 5 神社|editor=[[池田源太]]、[[宮坂敏和]]、奈良県史編集委員会|publisher=[[名著出版]]|year=1989|page=|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=奈良市史 社寺編|editor=奈良市史編集審議会|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=日本歴史地名大系. 第30巻 (奈良県の地名)|editor=|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1981|page=520}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * [[早良親王]] * [[璉珹寺]] * [[御霊神社 (奈良市)|御霊神社]] * [[御霊信仰]] * [[春日移し]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すとうてんのうしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]] [[Category:春日移し]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:奈良市の重要文化財]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|近代社格制度|" to "|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|"
Text replacement - "Former Village Shrines of" to "Former Village Shrines in"
Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
Bot: fix redirect [[Goryō Shrine (Nara City)]] → [[Goryō Shrine (Nara)]]
Bot: fix redirect [[Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime]] → [[Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime]]
Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Sōdō Emperor Shrine]]
Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|qq=}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=}} |founded = Traditionally [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], cypress-bark roof |annual festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing [[:en:Onryō|vengeful spirits]], and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|qq=}} entry for [[:en:1468|1468]] (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|qq=}} of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]], with its Buddhist counterpart being [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]]. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|qq=}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū Shrine]], and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The [[:en:Kagura-den|Kagura Hall]] was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable shrine]] storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|qq=}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meicho Publishing|qq=}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:奈良市の神社
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|qq=}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=}} |founded = Traditionally [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], cypress-bark roof |annual festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing [[:en:Onryō|vengeful spirits]], and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|qq=}} entry for [[:en:1468|1468]] (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|qq=}} of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]], with its Buddhist counterpart being [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]]. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|qq=}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū Shrine]], and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The [[:en:Kagura-den|Kagura Hall]] was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable shrine]] storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|qq=}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meicho Publishing|qq=}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} * [[:en:Goryō|Goryō]] * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Transference|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-奈良県}} {{translated page|ja|崇道天皇社|version=97044015|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga Transference]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (Buildings)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sōdō Emperor Shrine]] [[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[sn:ōdō Emperor Shrine]] [[en:Sōdō Emperor Shrine]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]]

According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing [[:en:Onryō|vengeful spirits]], and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|qq=}} entry for [[:en:1468|1468]] (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|qq=}} of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]], with its Buddhist counterpart being [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]]. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|qq=}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū Shrine]], and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The [[:en:Kagura-den|Kagura Hall]] was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable shrine]] storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|qq=}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite revision.comment: Bot: replace redirect category → Cultural heritage monuments in Japan
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|qq=}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=}} |founded = Traditionally [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], cypress-bark roof |annual festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing [[:en:Onryō|vengeful spirits]], and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|qq=}} entry for [[:en:1468|1468]] (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|qq=}} of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]], with its Buddhist counterpart being [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]]. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|qq=}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū Shrine]], and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The [[:en:Kagura-den|Kagura Hall]] was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable shrine]] storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|qq=}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meicho Publishing|qq=}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} * [[:en:Goryō|Goryō]] * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Transference|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-奈良県}} {{translated page|ja|崇道天皇社|version=97044015|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga Transference]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|qq=}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=}} |founded = Traditionally [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], cypress-bark roof |annual festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing [[:en:Onryō|vengeful spirits]], and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|qq=}} entry for [[:en:1468|1468]] (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|qq=}} of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]], with its Buddhist counterpart being [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]]. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|qq=}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|qq=}}, [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū Shrine]], and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The [[:en:Kagura-den|Kagura Hall]] was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable shrine]] storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|qq=}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite The [[:en:Kagura-den|Kagura Hall]] was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable shrine]] storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|qq=}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=}} |founded = Traditionally [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], cypress-bark roof |annual festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|qq=}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing [[:en:Onryō|vengeful spirits]], and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|qq=}} entry for [[:en:1468|1468]] (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|qq=}} of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]], with its Buddhist counterpart being [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]]. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|qq=}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. The [[:en:Kagura-den|Kagura Hall]] was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable shrine]] storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |founded = Traditionally [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], cypress-bark roof |annual festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing [[:en:Onryō|vengeful spirits]], and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|qq=}} entry for [[:en:1468|1468]] (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]], with its Buddhist counterpart being [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]]. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安土桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū Shrine]], and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The [[:en:Kagura-den|Kagura Hall]] was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable shrine]] storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 猩猩}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meicho Publishing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} * [[:en:Goryō|Goryō]] * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Transference|qq=}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-奈良県}} {{translated page|ja|崇道天皇社|version=97044015|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga Transference]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nara City]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sōdō Emperor Shrine]] [[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[sn:ōdō Emperor Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing [[:en:Onryō|vengeful spirits]], and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|qq=}} entry for [[:en:1468|1468]] (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]], with its Buddhist counterpart being [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]]. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安土桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū Shrine]], and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The [[:en:Kagura-den|Kagura Hall]] was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable shrine]] storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 猩猩}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing [[:en:Onryō|vengeful spirits]], and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|qq=}} entry for [[:en:1468|1468]] (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]], with its Buddhist counterpart being [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]]. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安土桃山時代}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū Shrine]], and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The [[:en:Kagura-den|Kagura Hall]] was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable shrine]] storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-Hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 猩猩}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography
