Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ([[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Traditionally [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], cypress-bark roof |annual festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in [[:en:806|806]] (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]]:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing [[:en:Onryō|vengeful spirits]], and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}} entry for [[:en:1468|1468]] (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of [[:en:Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga-taisha]], with its Buddhist counterpart being [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]]. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安土桃山時代|1=Azuchi-Momoyama period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q319531}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū Shrine]], and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The [[:en:Kagura-den|Kagura Hall]] was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The [[:en:Mikoshi|Portable shrine]] storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]], was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of [[:en:Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu]] and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 猩猩|1=Shōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3042028}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meicho Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sawara]] * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} * [[:en:Goryō|Goryō]] * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Transference|1=Kasuga utsushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865198}} [[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22119116]] Wikidata name: Sudō Tennō-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|en|Onryō|WD=Q634195|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}} entry for {{ill|1468|en|1468|WD=Q6564|lt=1468}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|en|Maitreya|WD=Q193461|lt=Maitreya}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安土桃山時代|1=Azuchi-Momoyama period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q319531}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū Shrine}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kagura Hall}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Portable shrine}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 猩猩|1=Shōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3042028}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meicho Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=Goryō}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Transference|1=Kasuga utsushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865198}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-奈良県}} {{translated page|ja|崇道天皇社|version=97044015|comment=Imported full ja history}} It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}:

It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|en|Onryō|WD=Q634195|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū Shrine}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kagura Hall}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Portable shrine}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi Transference|1=Kasuga utsushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865198}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-奈良県}} {{translated page|ja|崇道天皇社|version=97044015|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22119116]] Wikidata name: Sudō Tennō-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|en|Onryō|WD=Q634195|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}} entry for {{ill|1468|en|1468|WD=Q6564|lt=1468}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|en|Maitreya|WD=Q193461|lt=Maitreya}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安土桃山時代|1=Azuchi-Momoyama period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q319531}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū Shrine}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kagura Hall}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Portable shrine}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 猩猩|1=Shōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3042028}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meicho Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=Goryō}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Transference|1=Kasuga utsushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865198}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-奈良県}} {{translated page|ja|崇道天皇社|version=97044015|comment=Imported full ja history}} revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|en|Onryō|WD=Q634195|lt=vengeful spirits}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}} entry for {{ill|1468|en|1468|WD=Q6564|lt=1468}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|en|Maitreya|WD=Q193461|lt=Maitreya}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安土桃山時代|1=Azuchi-Momoyama period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q319531}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū Shrine}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kagura Hall}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Portable shrine}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 猩猩|1=Shōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3042028}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meicho Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara}} * {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|WD=Q3273210|lt=Goryō}} * {{ill|Kasuga Transference|ja|春日移し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Transference|1=Kasuga utsushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865198}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-奈良県}} {{translated page|ja|崇道天皇社|version=97044015|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806|1=806}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806|1=806}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|en|Onryō|WD=Q634195|lt=vengeful spirits|1=Onryō}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}} entry for {{ill|1468|en|1468|WD=Q6564|lt=1468|1=1468}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|en|Maitreya|WD=Q193461|lt=Maitreya|1=Maitreya}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安土桃山時代|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q319531}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū Shrine|1=Tenmangū}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kagura Hall|1=Kagura-den}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Portable shrine|1=Mikoshi}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|1=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * redirects to サルタヒコ}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 猩猩|1=Shōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3042028}} mask, and a lion dance his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 猩猩|1=Shōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3042028}} mask, and a lion dance [[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[co:commons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Emperor Sudo Shrine in Nara Kidera]][[ja:崇道天皇社]] {{Otheruses|Sudo Emperor Shrine in Nishikiji-chō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture|other shrines enshrining Prince Sawara (Sudo Emperor)|Sudo Tenno Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sudo Emperor Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nara Sudo-tenno-sha.jpg|270px]]
Sudo Emperor Shrine |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}{{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 40 |enshrined deity = Sudo Emperor ({{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}) |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |founded = Traditionally {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806|1=806}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign) |main hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} |cultural property = Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 23.99 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 50|long_sec = 00.90 |map = Nara city |other names = Kidera Tenno }} '''Sudo Emperor Shrine''' (崇道天皇社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nishikiji-chō|ja|西紀寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikiji-chō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.22. It is also referred to as "Kidera Tenno" and was historically the guardian shrine of {{ill|Renjōji Temple|ja|璉珹寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renjōji Temple|qq=}} (Kidera)Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.267. It is located northeast of the temple and is regarded alongside {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara)|lt=Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara City)|qq=}} in Yakushidō-chō as one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto"Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines, p.265. == History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been established in {{ill|806|en|806|WD=Q30021|lt=806|1=806}} (Daido 1, Emperor Heijō's reign)Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume, p.87. According to the 1458 document "Brief Historical Records of Reianji Goryō Daimyōjin" held by the Fujii family in Gojō, the shrine was established to pacify the spirit of {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}:
The deity enshrined at "Kidera Tenno" in the southern village of Nara is Sudo Emperor (Prince Sawara).
It is part of the {{ill|Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryo Shrine|lt=Goryō Shrines|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrines|qq=}} dedicated to a11easing {{ill|Onryō|en|Onryō|WD=Q634195|lt=vengeful spirits|1=Onryō}}, and {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}} ceremonies were held here as one of the Eight Goryō Shrines of NantoJapanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture, p.520. The {{ill|Daijōin Temple Records|ja|大乗院寺社雑事記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijōin Temple Records|1=Daijōin Jisha Zōjiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432101}} entry for {{ill|1468|en|1468|WD=Q6564|lt=1468|1=1468}} (Ōnin 2) notes that the shrine was a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/branch shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|WD=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}}, with its Buddhist counterpart being {{ill|Maitreya|en|Maitreya|WD=Q193461|lt=Maitreya|1=Maitreya}}. == Grounds == The main hall, designated as an Important Cultural Property, was originally built during the {{ill|Momoyama period|ja|桃山時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安土桃山時代|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q319531}} and relocated in 1623 (Genna 9) from the Kasuga Wakamiya Shrine. While the hall faces south today, it faced west until 1875 (Meiji 8), when the torii gate and front gate were reoriented. Auxiliary shrines on the grounds include the {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū Shrine|1=Tenmangū}}, and an Inari Shrine. According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kagura Hall|1=Kagura-den}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Portable shrine|1=Mikoshi}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|1=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi ** {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}} ** Hayasasurahime * Tenmangū Shrine ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇賀御魂神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * Main Hall === Other Cultural Properties === * Masks and costumes, including a {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} mask, a {{ill|Shōjō|ja|猩々|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 猩猩|1=Shōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3042028}} mask, and a lion dance head, are preserved. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History Vol. 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meicho Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}|year=1985|page=87}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place names Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1981|page=520}} == See Also == {{Commonscat}} {{osm box|w|342874002}} * {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince According to the Shrine Details Ledger (Meiji 24), these shrines were initially independent but are now combined with the Haraedo Shrine. The {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kagura Hall|1=Kagura-den}} was restored in 1855 (Ansei 2), and the Ritual House was completed in 1940 (Shōwa 15). The {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Portable shrine|1=Mikoshi}} storehouse houses a shrine repaired around 1717 (Kyōhō 2), which is used during the autumn festival. The 1,520 square meters of shrine grounds feature large sacred trees and a seat known as the "Kasuga Viewing Stone". == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, was named Crown Prince in 781 (Ten'ō 1) but was implicated in the assassination of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|WD=Q3090668|lt=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu|1=Fujiwara no Tanetsugu}} and deposed in 785 (Enryaku 4). While being exiled to Awaji Province, he passed away at age 49. His remains were reburied at {{ill|Yashima Mausoleum|ja|八島陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima Mausoleum|qq=}} in Yamato Province, and he was posthumously given the title "Sudo Emperor" to a11ease his spirit. === Auxiliary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine ** {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}} ** Kibuikidonushi
